root_text.sn47.15:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā bāhiyo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā bāhiyo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn47.15:1.3: “sādhu me, bhante, bhagavā saṅkhittena dhammaṁ desetu, yamahaṁ bhagavato dhammaṁ sutvā eko vūpakaṭṭho appamatto ātāpī pahitatto vihareyyan”ti.
root_text.sn47.15:1.4: “Tasmātiha tvaṁ, bāhiya, ādimeva visodhehi kusalesu dhammesu.
root_text.sn47.15:1.7: Yato ca kho te, bāhiya, sīlañca suvisuddhaṁ bhavissati, diṭṭhi ca ujukā, tato tvaṁ, bāhiya, sīlaṁ nissāya sīle patiṭṭhāya cattāro satipaṭṭhāne bhāveyyāsi.
root_text.sn47.15:2.2: Idha tvaṁ, bāhiya, kāye kāyānupassī viharāhi ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.15:2.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharāhi ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.15:2.6: Yato kho tvaṁ, bāhiya, sīlaṁ nissāya sīle patiṭṭhāya ime cattāro satipaṭṭhāne evaṁ bhāvessasi, tato tuyhaṁ, bāhiya, yā ratti vā divaso vā āgamissati, vuddhiyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu, no parihānī”ti.
root_text.sn47.15:3.1: Atha kho āyasmā bāhiyo bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinanditvā anumoditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi.
root_text.sn47.15:3.2: Atha kho āyasmā bāhiyo eko vūpakaṭṭho appamatto ātāpī pahitatto viharanto nacirasseva—yassatthāya kulaputtā sammadeva agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajanti, tadanuttaraṁ—brahmacariyapariyosānaṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihāsi.
root_text.sn47.15:3.3: “Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā”ti abbhaññāsi.
root_text.sn47.15:3.4: Aññataro ca panāyasmā bāhiyo arahataṁ ahosīti.

translation_text.sn47.15:1.2: Then Venerable Bāhiya went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn47.15:1.3: “Sir, may the Buddha please teach me Dhamma in brief. When I’ve heard it, I’ll live alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute.”
translation_text.sn47.15:1.4: “Well then, Bāhiya, you should purify the starting point of skillful qualities.
translation_text.sn47.15:1.5: What is the starting point of skillful qualities?
translation_text.sn47.15:1.6: Well purified ethics and correct view.
translation_text.sn47.15:1.7: When your ethics are well purified and your view is correct, you should develop the four kinds of mindfulness meditation, depending on and grounded on ethics.
translation_text.sn47.15:2.2: Meditate observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.15:2.3: Meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.15:2.5: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.15:2.6: When you develop these four kinds of mindfulness meditation, depending on and grounded on ethics, you can expect growth, not decline, in skillful qualities, whether by day or by night.”
translation_text.sn47.15:3.1: And then Venerable Bāhiya approved and agreed with what the Buddha said. He got up from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on his right, before leaving.
translation_text.sn47.15:3.2: Then Bāhiya, living alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute, soon realized the supreme end of the spiritual path in this very life. He lived having achieved with his own insight the goal for which gentlemen rightly go forth from the lay life to homelessness.
translation_text.sn47.15:3.3: He understood: “Rebirth is ended; the spiritual journey has been completed; what had to be done has been done; there is nothing further for this place.”
translation_text.sn47.15:3.4: And Venerable Bāhiya became one of the perfected.
